hints:
If-clause → Present Simple (without will). Verb: rain. Example: *If it rains tomorrow, we will stay indoors.*.
If-clause → Present Simple (without will). Verb: stay. Example: *If it rains tomorrow, we will stay indoors.*.

a1.rains: ✅ "rains" is the correct Present Simple form in the if part. Example: *If it rains tomorrow, we will stay indoors.*.
a1.will rain: ❌ In the subordinate clause of the first conditional, we do not use will/won't. See example: *If it rains tomorrow, we will stay indoors.*.
a1.is raining: ❌ The if-clause requires Present Simple, not the continuous tense. The correct example: *If it rains tomorrow, we will stay indoors.*.

a2.will stay: ✅ "will stay" — the correct form of Present Simple in the if clause. Example: *If it rains tomorrow, we will stay indoors.*.
a2.stays: ❌ Try Present Simple in the if clause. Correct example: *If it rains tomorrow, we will stay indoors.*.
a2.are staying: ❌ The if-clause requires Present Simple, not the continuous tense. The correct example: *If it rains tomorrow, we will stay indoors.*.

hints:
Main clause → will + V1 / won't + V1 for the result. Verb: not go. Example: *We won't go to the beach if it is cold.*.
If-clause → Present Simple (without will). Verb: be. Example: *We won't go to the beach if it is cold.*.

a1.won't go: ✅ "won't go" — the correct form of Future Simple in the main clause. Example: *We won't go to the beach if it is cold.*.
a1.don't go: ❌ Negation with do/does is not suitable; will/won't is needed. Look at the example: *We won't go to the beach if it is cold.*.
a1.aren't going: ❌ It's better to use will/won't + be, as in the example: *We won't go to the beach if it is cold.*.

a2.is: ✅ "is" — the correct form of Present Simple in the if clause. Example: *We won't go to the beach if it is cold.*.
a2.will be: ❌ In the subordinate clause of the first conditional, we do not use will/won't. See the example: *We won't go to the beach if it is cold.*.
a2.was: ❌ The first conditional after if uses the present tense, not the past. Example: *We won't go to the beach if it is cold.*.

a2.won't attend: ✅ "won't attend" — the correct form of Present Simple in the if clause. Example: *If he works late, he won't attend the meeting.*.
a2.doesn't attend: ❌ Try Present Simple in the if clause. Correct example: *If he works late, he won't attend the meeting.*.

If-clause → Present Simple (without will). Verb: apologize. Example: *She won't be upset if you apologize.*.

a1.won't be: ✅ "won't be" — the correct form of Future Simple in the main clause. Example: *She won't be upset if you apologize.*.
a1.isn't: ❌ It's better to use will/won't + be, as in the example: *She won't be upset if you apologize.*.
a1.will not be: ❌ "will not be" is grammatically possible, but the task expects the contraction "won't be". Example: *She won't be upset if you apologize.*.

a2.apologize: ✅ "apologize" — the correct form of Present Simple in the if clause. Example: *She won't be upset if you apologize.*.
